timeline.description: Initial tribe formation with nine pairs of loved ones split into two tribes
timeline.impact: 18 castaways (9 loved ones pairs) divided into two tribes

timeline.description: Blood vs. Water format with Exile Island twist - pairs of loved ones split across tribes

details.mechanic: Nine pairs of loved ones competing against each other
details.exile_island: Returns after 11-season hiatus, replacing originally planned Redemption Island
details.challenge_format: Reward Challenges are one-on-one duels between loved ones

timeline.impact: Loved ones competing against each other created unique strategic dynamics

timeline.description: John Rocker blindsided by Josh's alliance on Coyopa with idol in his pocket

details.action: Josh orchestrated John's ouster, flipping from the men's alliance
details.reason: John's verbal confrontations with other tribe after challenges made him a liability
details.idol_status: John was eliminated with an unused Hidden Immunity Idol

timeline.impact: First major blindside of the season; Josh gained control of Coyopa

timeline.description: Tribe swap redistributed remaining 14 castaways into new Coyopa and Hunahpu

tribes_after_swap.name: Coyopa
tribes_after_swap.composition: Mix of original Coyopa and Hunahpu members
tribes_after_swap.notes: Missy came into power on new Coyopa

tribes_after_swap.name: Hunahpu
tribes_after_swap.composition: Mix of original Coyopa and Hunahpu members
tribes_after_swap.notes: Josh and Jeremy vied for control; Hunahpu won all remaining pre-merge immunity challenges

timeline.impact: Power dynamics shifted; Josh and Jeremy's conflict set up but never resolved pre-merge due to immunity wins

timeline.description: Tribes merged into single tribe with 11 remaining castaways
timeline.impact: Two factions emerged: Jeremy's alliance (Jeremy, Natalie, Julie, Missy, Baylor) vs. Josh's alliance (Josh, Reed, Wes, Keith, Alec), with Jon and Jaclyn as swing votes

timeline.description: Julie McGee quit the game after trail mix scandal and missing boyfriend John Rocker
timeline.reason: Trail mix hoarding scandal combined with longing for boyfriend John Rocker and backlash from his controversial elimination
timeline.impact: Tribal Council was cancelled; gave Jon and Jaclyn time to flip to Jeremy's alliance; Josh eliminated at next vote
timeline.notable: Only quit in Season 29; quit occurred on Day 18 of 39

timeline.description: Josh Canfield blindsided after Jon and Jaclyn flipped alliances

details.action: Jon and Jaclyn sided with Jeremy's alliance to eliminate Josh
details.context: Julie's quit gave Jon and Jaclyn time to reconsider their alliance with Josh

timeline.impact: Major power shift; Jeremy's alliance took control of the game

timeline.description: Jeremy Collins blindsided by Jon's alliance with Natalie's help

details.action: Jon's alliance blindsided Jeremy in a 5-3-2 vote
details.natalie_role: Natalie helped vote out her closest ally Jeremy to avoid suspicion
details.motivation: Natalie biding her time for the right moment to retaliate and avenge Jeremy

timeline.impact: Jeremy eliminated as strategic threat; Natalie began planning revenge against Jon

timeline.description: Natalie Anderson found Hidden Immunity Idol with Baylor's help

details.method: Drafted Baylor during reward when camp was empty, searched together

timeline.impact: Natalie gained protection and power for upcoming moves

timeline.description: Double idol play at Tribal Council - Jon and Keith both played idols

details.action: Jon and Keith both played Hidden Immunity Idols at same Tribal Council
details.votes_negated: Four votes against Jon and three votes against Keith were not counted
details.natalie_role: Natalie implored Jon to play his idol, which saved him
details.keith_action: Keith offered his idol to son Wes, who refused; Keith played it for himself
details.result: Wes Nale became first castaway voted out as result of two idols being played

timeline.impact: Wes eliminated despite both Jon and Keith safe; dramatic Tribal Council moment

timeline.description: Jon Misch blindsided by Natalie's alliance with idol in his pocket

details.action: Natalie spearheaded Jon's elimination in revenge for Jeremy's blindside
details.idol_status: Jon was eliminated with an unused Hidden Immunity Idol

timeline.impact: Natalie's revenge move; Jon became second player this season eliminated with idol in pocket

timeline.description: Natalie played Hidden Immunity Idol on Jaclyn to save her

details.action: Natalie played idol on Jaclyn, negating three votes against her

timeline.impact: Natalie solidified Jaclyn's loyalty; demonstrated strategic control

timeline.description: Final Tribal Council and jury vote

details.reed_speech: Reed eviscerated Missy's character at Final Tribal Council
details.jaclyn_critique: Questioned for only making moves with others, primarily Jon
details.natalie_praise: Praised for making bold moves without a loved one's support

timeline.impact: Natalie Anderson crowned Sole Survivor and winner of $1,000,000

season_mechanics.description: Nine pairs of loved ones competing against each other, split across two tribes
Returns after 11-season hiatus; Reward Challenges feature one-on-one duels between loved ones
Day 11 swap redistributed 14 remaining castaways into new Coyopa and Hunahpu tribes
Standard Hidden Immunity Idols available throughout the game

pre_game_notes.original_cast_change: Sisters So Kim and Doo Kim were originally cast but dropped out before the game began due to a medical emergency
pre_game_notes.gender_imbalance: Resulted in uneven gender balance with 10 males and 8 females competing
